question.label: Excellent examples
question.name: What examples of excellent junior-level work would make the bar clearer for you?
question.description: Drills into the specific examples he says are missing so the quality bar becomes concrete.

answer: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.

assessment.note: He describes broad reviews leaving him unsure what to change first — a strong clarity gap, with mild strain from not knowing where to focus; wellbeing has 0 touches after 3+ turns, so next question must include it.

new_queue.label: Expectation gap
new_queue.name: Where do my expectations for your work feel different from yours right now?

new_queue.label: Quality friction
new_queue.name: What part of the quality bar feels hardest to apply consistently?

new_queue.label: Growth blocker
new_queue.name: What is getting in the way of the next skill you want to build?

new_queue.label: Two-week focus
new_queue.name: What's the first concrete change you want to make before we meet again?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
